entry.title: Bitcoin covenants are inevitable
entry.summary: The discussion on blockchain security by Eric Voskuil highlights the limitations of existing mechanisms against double-spend and 51% attacks when large amounts are involved. He suggests that reducing the block size could enhance security against these threats. This approach would increase fee pressure, which in turn might bolster double-spend security while simultaneously alleviating some operational pressures faced by node operators. Moreover, Voskuil indicates that changes to inflation rates within the blockchain framework are unlikely to be considered as a viable solution. This stance points towards a strategic focus on adjusting technical parameters rather than altering the economic foundations of blockchain technology to improve security.
